name: Vanuatu
_editorial_notes: Researched 2026-07-21 from official .gov.vu sources plus a few closely-linked official sites (vfsc.vu, tradeportal.gov.vu, vqa.edu.vu, scholarships.gov.vu). Not rendered by the app -- kept here for future maintainers.

moet.gov.vu and education.gov.vu (Ministry of Education and Training) were unreachable during research (connection timeouts). MoET's contact info below comes from a Wayback Machine snapshot dated 2025-09-01, not a live fetch -- worth re-verifying once the site is reachable.

The foreign-investment approval body is 'Vanuatu Foreign Investment Promotion Agency (VFIPA)' at investvanuatu.vu, though the government's own Trade Portal still uses the older name 'Vanuatu Investment Promotion Authority (VIPA)' with a vipa.org.vu email -- looks like an incomplete rebrand. No evidence was found that 'VIRO' (a name that shows up in some casual references) is or ever was this body's actual name -- deliberately not used here.

moia.gov.vu's own homepage HTML contains a leftover template artifact: a fake US address/phone/email ('4517 Washington Ave. Manchester, Kentucky', '(201) 555-0124') sitting in the page markup near the real Vanuatu contact info. Obviously not real MoIA contact info -- excluded entirely, real MoIA contact used instead.

Several contact numbers below are sourced from web-search summaries rather than a directly-fetched-and-quoted government page (Lands Department phone, VAT office phone, Northern Provincial Hospital phone, Vila Central Hospital phone) -- treated as reasonably reliable but not verbatim-confirmed. A phone call to confirm before treating as fully final would be ideal.

A 'Torba provincial hospital' was mentioned in one non-government secondary source but could not be corroborated on moh.gov.vu -- deliberately excluded rather than guessed. Norsup Hospital (Malekula) and Lolowai Hospital (Ambae) are confirmed to exist and their island location is confirmed, but specific contact numbers for either were not found -- included with location only.

items.name: Passport (new or renewal)
items.agency: Department of Immigration and Passport Services, Ministry of Internal Affairs
items.location: Kumul Highway, Tamarama Building, Port Vila
items.description: Normal processing (up to 21 days): VT 10,000. Express (up to 10 days): VT 20,000. Urgent (up to 3 days): VT 25,000. Children under 18 pay less. Provincial lodging points also exist in Sanma, Penama, and Tafea.

items.name: Birth, marriage, and death certificates
items.agency: Civil Registry and Identity Management (CRIM), Ministry of Internal Affairs
items.contact: (678) 33200 (Ministry of Internal Affairs, Independence Park, Port Vila)
items.description: Civil registration records for births, deaths, and marriages -- needed as a prerequisite for a passport application.

items.name: Business name / company registration
items.location: Companies House, Rue Bougainville, Port Vila (also a Santo office in Luganville)
items.description: Business names can be reserved for up to 6 months. Registration can be done online, at an in-person kiosk, or on paper. Annual returns must be filed to keep details current.

items.name: Foreign investment approval
items.agency: Vanuatu Foreign Investment Promotion Agency (VFIPA, formerly VIPA)
items.description: Required for foreign-owned businesses, after VFSC business registration and before a business license can be issued.

items.name: Land registry and leases
items.agency: Department of Lands, Survey and Registry, Ministry of Lands and Natural Resources
items.description: Handles lease registration, land surveys, and valuation. Lease records have been digital since 2007.

items.name: Driver's license
items.agency: Vanuatu Police Force Traffic Section (driving test) and Department of Customs and Inland Revenue (license issuance)
items.description: License fee VT 5,000, valid 3 years; renewal VT 5,000; lost/stolen re-issue VT 2,500. Visiting drivers with a foreign license can drive for up to 3 months before needing a local one.

items.name: Tax / VAT registration
items.agency: Department of Customs and Inland Revenue
items.description: VAT is 15%. Registration is required once taxable turnover exceeds VT 4 million/year; optional below that.

items.name: Ministry of Health
items.description: Governs Vanuatu's health system: hospitals, health centres, dispensaries, and aid posts.

items.name: Vila Central Hospital
items.description: The country's main national referral hospital.

items.name: Northern Provincial Hospital (also called Northern District Hospital)
items.description: Referral hospital for Luganville and the four northern provinces (Malampa, Penama, Sanma, Torba) -- about half the country's population.

items.name: Lenakel Hospital
items.description: Provincial referral hospital for Tafea Province.

items.name: Ministry of Education and Training (MoET)
items.description: Runs national schooling and the national examinations. Also maintains a national School Mapping tool showing every school's location.

items.name: Teaching Service Commission (TSC)
items.description: Handles teacher licensing and employment matters.

items.name: Vanuatu Qualifications Authority (VQA)
items.description: Oversees the Vanuatu National Qualifications Framework and post-secondary/TVET training (course accreditation, provider registration).

items.name: Vanuatu Government Scholarships
items.description: Applications go through the SMIS online portal (scholarships.gov.vu) -- no paper applications accepted. Covers Year 12/13 students and several priority-sector categories.

sources.label: Department of Immigration and Passport Services
Ministry of Lands and Natural Resources
Department of Customs and Inland Revenue
